pageSubtitle: Step-by-step guides for every module in Ozzyl HMS

labels.howToUse: ✅ How to Use — Step by Step

login.title: Login & Access
login.where: Go to your hospital's URL → Login page
steps: Open your hospital's URL (e.g. /h/your-hospital/login)
Enter your registered Email address
Enter your Password and click 'Sign In'
You will be automatically taken to the correct dashboard based on your role
If you forgot your password, ask the Hospital Admin to reset it for you
tips: Bookmark your hospital's login URL for quick access
Use a strong password with letters, numbers, and symbols
You can switch between English and Bangla using the language toggle in the sidebar
warnings: Do not share your login credentials with others
Log out after finishing your session on shared computers
If login fails, check that CAPS LOCK is not on

navigation.title: Dashboard & Navigation
navigation.where: Sidebar on the left side of every page
steps: After logging in, you will see your role-based dashboard
The left sidebar shows all available modules for your role
Modules are grouped into sections: Operations, Clinical, Finance, Administration
Click any menu item to navigate to that module
On mobile, tap the hamburger menu (≡) at top-left to open the sidebar
Click the language button (EN/বাং) to switch between English and Bangla
Click the moon/sun icon in the top-right header to toggle dark mode
Your name and role appear in the top-right header; click to sign out
tips: The active page is highlighted in blue in the sidebar
Use the browser back button to return to the previous page
warnings: If a menu item is missing, your role may not have permission — contact admin
Refreshing the page will keep you logged in (your session is preserved)

settings.title: Hospital Settings
steps: Click 'Settings' from the sidebar
Update Hospital Name, Address, Phone, Email, and Logo
Set the default currency, time zone, and working hours
Configure tax rates and billing preferences
Click 'Save Changes' to apply your settings
tips: Add a proper hospital logo for professional bill printing
Set your correct time zone to ensure timestamps are accurate
warnings: Changing the hospital name will update it everywhere including printed bills
Only Hospital Admin can change system settings

appointments.title: Appointments
steps: Click 'Appointments' from the sidebar
Click '+ New Appointment' button
Search for the patient by name or phone number; or create a new patient
Select the doctor, date, and available time slot
Add any notes or reason for visit
Click 'Save' to book the appointment
To reschedule: click on an existing appointment → 'Edit' → change date/time → Save
To cancel: click on an appointment → 'Cancel' → confirm
tips: Use the calendar view to see all appointments for the day
Colour coding shows appointment status: Pending (yellow), Confirmed (green), Cancelled (red)
warnings: Do not double-book the same time slot for one doctor
Always confirm with the patient before cancelling an appointment

patients.title: Patient Management
steps: Click 'Patients' from the sidebar to see all registered patients
Use the search bar to find a patient by name, phone, or patient ID
Click '+ New Patient' to register a new patient
Fill in: Name, Date of Birth, Gender, Phone, Address, Blood Group
Click 'Save' to create the patient record
Click on any patient name to see their full detail and history
From the patient detail page you can see: visits, prescriptions, lab results, billing history, vitals, allergies
Click 'Timeline' tab to see the patient's full medical journey
tips: Always search before adding a new patient to avoid duplicate records
Patient ID is auto-generated — note it for future reference
Use the patient Timeline to get a quick overview of their entire history
warnings: Never create duplicate patient records — search thoroughly first
Only update patient information with verified data

emergency.title: Emergency Department
steps: Click 'Emergency' from the sidebar
Click '+ New Emergency Case' to register an emergency patient
Enter patient details and chief complaint (main problem)
Assign triage level: Critical, Urgent, or Non-Urgent
Assign the treating doctor and emergency bed
Monitor the patient's status from the emergency dashboard
Update patient condition as treatment progresses
When resolved, click 'Discharge' or 'Admit to IPD' as needed
tips: Critical cases (red) must be attended to immediately
Use the dashboard to track all active emergency cases at a glance
warnings: Always assign a triage level — do not leave it blank
Discharge only after the doctor confirms the patient is stable

ot.title: Operation Theatre (OT)
steps: Click 'Operation Theatre' from the sidebar
Click '+ Schedule Operation' to book a new surgery
Select the patient (must be admitted as IPD first)
Choose the operation type, surgeon, anesthesiologist, and OT room
Set the scheduled date and time
Add pre-operative notes and special requirements
Click 'Save' to confirm the schedule
On the day of surgery, click 'Start Operation' to begin tracking
After surgery, click 'Complete' and enter post-operative notes
tips: Check bed and OT room availability before scheduling
Add all team members (scrub nurse, anesthetist) in the schedule
warnings: Patient must be admitted to IPD before scheduling an operation
Confirm all blood and equipment availability before surgery

admissions.title: IPD / Admissions
steps: Click 'IPD / Admissions' from the sidebar
Click '+ New Admission' to admit a patient
Search and select the patient
Select the ward, room, and bed (from available beds)
Enter the admitting doctor and diagnosis
Set the expected length of stay
Click 'Admit' to confirm
The patient will now appear in the active admissions list
To discharge: find the patient → click 'Discharge' → fill discharge summary → confirm
tips: Check bed availability from the Bed Management page before admitting
The patient's billing will automatically start from admission date
warnings: Do not admit a patient without a bed assignment
Always verify the patient's identity before admission

beds.title: Bed Management
steps: Click 'Beds' from the sidebar to see the full bed map
Beds are color-coded: Green = Available, Red = Occupied, Yellow = Under Maintenance
Click on any bed to see details of who is occupying it
To mark a bed under maintenance: click the bed → 'Set Maintenance' → Save
Beds are automatically freed when a patient is discharged
tips: Check this page before admitting new patients to find available beds
Group beds by ward for easier navigation
warnings: Only mark a bed as maintenance-free after it has been fully cleaned
Do not manually free a bed — it is handled automatically at discharge

nurseStation.title: Nurse Station
steps: Click 'Nurse Station' from the sidebar
View current admitted patients assigned to your ward
Click on a patient to see their orders, medications, and vitals
Enter vital signs (blood pressure, temperature, pulse) by clicking 'Record Vitals'
View and execute doctor's medication orders from the medication section
Mark tasks as completed once done
Use the notes section to add nursing observations
tips: Check this page at the beginning of every shift for pending tasks
Record vitals at regular intervals as per doctor's orders
warnings: Never skip recording vitals for critical patients
Always double-check medication doses before administering

nursing.title: Enhanced Nursing
steps: Click 'Enhanced Nursing' from the sidebar
Select the patient from the patient list
Use the 10 tabs: Care Plan, Notes, MAR, I/O Chart, Monitoring, IV Drugs, Wound Care, Handover, OPD Check-in, Patient Preferences
CARE PLAN: create nursing diagnoses and intervention plans
MAR (Medication Administration Record): record every medication given — time, dose, route
I/O CHART: record fluid intake (IV, oral) and output (urine, drainage) to calculate balance
WOUND CARE: document wound assessment, dressing type, and healing progress
HANDOVER: write shift handover notes for the incoming nurse
Click 'Save' after each entry
tips: MAR must be updated every time a medication is administered
I/O balance should be calculated at least every 8 hours
Handover notes ensure continuity of care between shifts
warnings: Never leave MAR entries blank — incomplete records are a safety risk
Wound care documentation must include measurement in centimeters (L x W x D)
Only clinical staff (doctors, nurses) can enter nursing records

doctorSchedule.title: Doctor Schedule
steps: Click 'Doctor Schedule' from the sidebar
Select a doctor from the dropdown to view their schedule
Click '+ Add Slot' to create a new availability slot
Choose the day of the week and time range
Set the maximum number of patients for that slot
Click 'Save' to publish the schedule
Patients will be able to book appointments within these slots
tips: Set up doctor schedules before allowing appointment bookings
Block out holidays by removing slots for those days
warnings: Deleting a schedule slot will not cancel existing appointments
Coordinate with doctors before modifying their schedules

telemedicine.title: Telemedicine
steps: Click 'Telemedicine' from the sidebar
View scheduled telemedicine appointments for today
Click on an appointment and then 'Start Session' to begin the video consultation
The patient will be automatically connected to the video room
During the session: use chat, share screen, or write prescriptions
After session: click 'End Session' and write consultation notes
The prescription and notes are saved to the patient's record automatically
tips: Test your camera and microphone before starting a session
Ensure a stable internet connection for clear video quality
warnings: Do not close the browser tab during a live session
Patient must have the session link or ID to join the call

lab.title: Laboratory / Tests
steps: Click 'Lab / Tests' from the sidebar
View all pending test orders and incoming samples
To create a test order: click '+ New Order' → select patient → choose tests
Print the sample collection label if needed
Once sample is received: click the order → 'Enter Results'
Input result values for each test parameter
Click 'Publish Results' to make them available to the doctor
Click 'Print Report' to generate the lab report for the patient
tips: Abnormal values are automatically highlighted in red
Reference ranges are set in Lab Settings — keep them updated
warnings: Double-check patient name and sample ID before entering results
Never publish results without verifying all values are entered correctly

pharmacy.title: Pharmacy
steps: Click 'Pharmacy' from the sidebar
View pending prescription orders from doctors
Click on a prescription to see the medicines prescribed
Check stock availability for each medicine
If in stock: click 'Dispense' → confirm quantities → click 'Complete'
If out of stock: mark as 'Out of Stock' and notify the doctor
Print dispensing label for each medicine packet
The patient's prescription is updated as dispensed
tips: Regularly update medicine stock levels to avoid shortages
Use the search bar to quickly find specific medicines
warnings: Always verify patient's allergies before dispensing any medicine
Never dispense expired medicines — check expiry dates

inventory.title: Inventory & Supply Chain
steps: Click 'Inventory' from the sidebar to see the inventory dashboard
STOCK: view current stock levels for all items — red = low stock
PURCHASE ORDER (PO): click '+ New PO' → select vendor → add items with quantity → submit
GOODS RECEIPT (GR): when goods arrive, go to GR → '+ New GR' → link to PO → confirm received items
REQUISITION: departments request items by submitting a Requisition form
DISPATCH: approve requisitions and dispatch items to departments
STOCK ADJUSTMENT: correct quantities if there is a discrepancy (theft, damage, counting error)
LEDGER: view full movement history of any item — all in/out transactions
tips: Set minimum stock alerts for critical items
Always link a GR to a PO to maintain accurate records
warnings: Stock adjustments require a valid reason — all changes are audited
Do not receive more quantities than what was ordered in the PO

vitals.title: Vitals
steps: Click 'Vitals' from the sidebar
Search for the patient by name or ID
Click '+ Record Vitals' to enter new readings
Enter: Blood Pressure (systolic/diastolic), Temperature, Pulse/Heart Rate, Respiratory Rate, SpO2, Weight, Height, BMI
Click 'Save' to record the vitals
View the trends graph to see changes over time
Values outside normal range are automatically flagged in red
tips: Record vitals at regular intervals for monitoring patients
BMI is auto-calculated once you enter weight and height
warnings: Critically abnormal vitals must be reported to the doctor immediately
Enter values in the correct units (e.g., temperature in °C, not °F)

allergies.title: Allergies
steps: Click 'Allergies' from the sidebar
Search for the patient
Click '+ Add Allergy' to record a new allergy
Enter: Allergen name, Reaction type (mild/moderate/severe/anaphylaxis), Onset date
Click 'Save'
The allergy will now appear as a warning whenever this patient's record is accessed
To update or remove an allergy: click on it → Edit or Delete
tips: Always check this section before prescribing or dispensing medicines
Record all known allergies even if they seem minor
warnings: Anaphylaxis allergies are life-threatening — always double-check
Drug allergies are especially important — the E-Prescribing module will automatically warn you

ePrescribing.title: E-Prescribing & Drug Safety
steps: Click 'E-Prescribing' from the sidebar
SAFETY CHECKER tab: select a patient → search for a drug → the system checks for interactions with the patient's current medications and allergies
If an interaction is found, a warning is shown with severity (High/Medium/Low)
A clinical staff member can override a warning with a justification note
DRUG CATALOG tab: browse the complete formulary (approved drug list)
Search drugs by name, category, or generic name
View: dose, route, frequency, contraindications, and interactions
INTERACTIONS tab: view all known drug-drug interaction pairs in the database
tips: Always run a safety check before prescribing a new drug to a patient on multiple medications
High severity interactions should almost never be overridden
warnings: The safety checker is a decision-support tool — not a replacement for clinical judgment
Keep the formulary updated by adding new drugs approved by the hospital
ePrescribing.roles: Hospital Admin, Doctors, Pharmacists (clinical roles only)

labSettings.title: Lab Settings
steps: Click 'Lab Settings' from the sidebar
TEST CATALOG: create and manage lab test definitions
Click '+ New Test' → enter test name, code, department, specimen type
Set price for billing purposes
REFERENCE RANGES: for each test, set normal ranges by age group and gender
PANELS: group multiple tests into a panel (e.g. 'CBC Panel' includes 12 tests)
Click 'Save' after each change
tips: Keep reference ranges updated with clinically approved values
Use panels to speed up ordering common test groups
warnings: Changing test prices will affect new bills only, not already-created bills
Deleting a test will fail if there are pending orders for that test

billing.title: Billing
steps: Click 'Billing' from the sidebar
Click '+ New Bill' to create a bill
Search and select the patient
Add billing items (consultation, medicines, tests, procedures) from the catalogue
Quantities and prices are auto-filled from Billing Master
Apply discounts if needed (requires admin approval for large discounts)
Select payment method: Cash, Card, Mobile Banking (bKash/Nagad), Insurance
Click 'Save & Pay' to finalize the bill
Click 'Print' to print the receipt for the patient
tips: Bills for admitted patients should be done through IP Billing, not the regular billing
Use Provisional Billing for long-stay patients to show interim totals
warnings: Double-check all items before finalizing — a bill cannot be easily edited after saving
Do not close the page before clicking Save

billingMaster.title: Billing Master
steps: Click 'Billing Master' from the sidebar
This is where all billable services and their prices are configured
ITEM CATEGORIES: create categories (e.g. Consultation, Surgery, Lab, Medicine)
ITEMS: add individual services/products under each category with a fixed price
GL PACKAGE: link billing items to accounting GL codes
DOCTOR ITEMS: assign consultation fees per doctor
Click 'Save' after adding or editing items
tips: Keep prices updated — all new bills will use the current price from Billing Master
Use consistent naming so billing staff can find items easily
warnings: Only admin should modify the Billing Master — prices affect all future bills
Deleting an item will fail if it has been used in existing bills

provisionalBilling.title: Provisional Billing
steps: Click 'Provisional Billing' from the sidebar
View all IPD patients with running provisional bills
A provisional bill accumulates charges during the patient's stay
Click on a patient to view all accrued charges (bed, medicines, tests, procedures)
Print the provisional bill to show the patient or family the estimated total
When the patient is discharged, click 'Convert to Final Bill' to create the actual invoice
tips: Share provisional bills with patient families regularly for long-stay IPD cases
All IPD charges (pharmacy, lab, procedures) are automatically added to the provisional bill
warnings: A provisional bill is NOT a receipt — the patient has not paid yet
Do not skip converting to Final Bill at discharge

deposits.title: Deposits
steps: Click 'Deposits' from the sidebar
Click '+ Collect Deposit' to record an advance payment from a patient
Search and select the patient
Enter amount and payment method
Click 'Save' — a deposit receipt is generated
The deposit will be automatically applied against the final bill at discharge
To refund: find the deposit → click 'Refund' → enter amount → confirm
tips: Always collect a deposit for IPD admissions
Keep deposit receipts for patient reference
warnings: Refunded deposits reduce the patient's credit balance immediately
Never refund more than the deposit amount

creditNotes.title: Credit Notes
steps: Click 'Credit Notes' from the sidebar
Credit notes are issued when a patient is overcharged or a charge needs to be reversed
Click '+ New Credit Note'
Select the original bill → enter the amount and reason
Click 'Issue Credit Note'
The credit note will reduce the patient's outstanding balance or be refunded
tips: Always document the reason for issuing a credit note
Credit notes create an accounting record — they are reversals, not direct refunds
warnings: Only admin should approve large credit notes
Credit notes are audited — always provide a valid reason

billing_handover.title: Bill Handover
steps: Click 'Bill Handover' from the sidebar
This is used when a billing counter transfers its cash collections to another counter or to accounts
Click '+ New Handover'
Select the source counter and destination
Enter the amount being handed over and the payment breakdown (cash, cards, etc.)
Both parties must confirm — click 'Submit Handover'
A handover receipt is generated for both parties
tips: Do handovers at the end of every shift
Count the cash before and after to verify accuracy
warnings: Discrepancies must be reported immediately and not ignored
Both sender and receiver must be present to confirm the handover

billing_cancellation.title: Bill Cancellation
steps: Click 'Bill Cancellation' from the sidebar
Search for the bill you want to cancel by bill number or patient name
Review the bill details
Click 'Cancel Bill' and enter the reason for cancellation
Submit for admin approval if required
Once approved, the bill is marked as cancelled and removed from outstanding
A cancellation receipt is generated for the audit trail
tips: Always cancel and reissue rather than editing a bill
The original bill remains in the system for audit purposes
warnings: Bill cancellations are irreversible — be absolutely sure before cancelling
Payments already collected must be refunded separately after cancellation

settlements.title: Settlements
steps: Click 'Settlements' from the sidebar
View all outstanding payments from patients or insurance companies
Click '+ New Settlement' to settle an outstanding amount
Select the patient, link to the bill
Enter the settlement amount and method (cash, bank transfer, insurance)
Click 'Settle'
The bill status is updated to 'Settled' and removed from outstanding
tips: Use settlements for insurance or delayed payments
Print the settlement receipt for the payer
warnings: Do not mark as settled without receiving the actual payment
Partial settlements are allowed — the remaining amount stays outstanding

accounting.title: Accounting
steps: Click 'Accounting' from the sidebar to see the accounting dashboard
The dashboard shows: total income, expenses, net profit, cash flow summary
Use the date filter to see figures for a specific period
Navigate to sub-sections: Income, Expenses, Chart of Accounts, Journal Entries, P&L, Recurring
tips: Review the dashboard at the end of each month to track financial health
Export reports for sharing with the Board or external accountants
warnings: Accounting data is automatically generated from billing, income, and expense entries — avoid manual overrides
Only authorized finance staff should access accounting records

income.title: Income
steps: Click 'Income' from the sidebar
View all income transactions with date, category, amount, and source
Click '+ New Income' to record a non-billing income (e.g. donations, grants, rent)
Enter: amount, category, description, date
Click 'Save'
Use filters to see income by category or date range
tips: Most billing income is auto-recorded — use this only for non-billing income sources
Keep income categories consistent for accurate reporting
warnings: Do not duplicate entries already recorded through billing
Always add a description for non-standard income entries

expenses.title: Expenses
steps: Click 'Expenses' from the sidebar
View all recorded expenses (salaries, utilities, supplies, etc.)
Click '+ New Expense' to record a new expense
Enter: amount, category, vendor, description, date, optional receipt
Click 'Save'
Use the Recurring option for monthly expenses like rent, salaries
tips: Upload receipts or invoices for proper documentation
Categorize expenses consistently — this affects the P&L report
warnings: Avoid duplicate expense entries — search first before adding
Salary expenses must be approved by management before recording

chartOfAccounts.title: Chart of Accounts
steps: Click 'Accounts' from the sidebar
View the full list of accounting GL codes organized by type (Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, Expenses, Equity)
Click '+ New Account' to add a new GL account
Enter: account code, name, type, parent account
Click 'Save'
Once accounts are set up, they can be linked to billing items and journal entries
tips: Follow standard accounting code conventions (1000s = Assets, 2000s = Liabilities, etc.)
Use parent accounts to create a hierarchy for cleaner reporting
warnings: Only accountants should modify the Chart of Accounts
Deleting an account that has transactions linked to it will cause errors

insurance.title: Insurance Claims
steps: Click 'Insurance' from the sidebar
View all submitted insurance claims and their status
Click '+ New Claim' to create a new insurance claim
Select the patient and their insurance policy
Link the associated bills/services
Enter claim amount, diagnosis codes, and attach documents if needed
Click 'Submit Claim'
Track claim status: Pending → Approved → Paid or Rejected
tips: Keep patient insurance information up-to-date in their profile
Rejected claims can be resubmitted after fixing the issue
warnings: Never submit a claim for services not actually rendered
Insurance fraud is illegal — report any suspicious activity to management

insuranceBilling.title: Insurance Billing
steps: Click 'Insurance Billing' from the sidebar
View all IPD patients whose bills will be paid by insurance
Select a patient → view their accrued charges
Verify which services are covered by the insurance policy
Generate the insurance claim invoice
Submit the claim digitally or print for manual submission
Track payment from the insurance company
tips: Know the insurance coverage limits before admitting the patient
Pre-authorize expensive procedures with the insurance company in advance
warnings: Insurance billing discrepancies lead to claim rejections — be accurate
Each insurance company has different claim formats — check their requirements

ipBilling.title: IP Billing (Inpatient Billing)
steps: Click 'IP Billing' from the sidebar
View all currently admitted inpatients and their accumulating charges
Click on a patient to see their itemized billing breakdown
Charges include: bed charges (daily), doctor visits, medicines, procedures, tests
Charges from pharmacy, lab, and nursing are auto-added when services are provided
When patient is ready for discharge: click 'Generate Final Bill'
Verify all charges, apply deposits/credit, collect remaining payment
Print the final inpatient bill receipt
tips: Review running totals periodically and share with patient families
All services rendered to the patient update this bill in real-time
warnings: Never finalize IP billing until all discharge procedures are completed
Verify that all dispensed medicines from pharmacy are reflected in the bill

payments.title: Payments
steps: Click 'Payments' from the sidebar
View all payment transactions recorded in the system
Filter by date range, payment method, or status
Click on any payment to see its details
Payments are automatically recorded when a bill is paid
For manual payment recording: click '+ New Payment' → select bill → enter amount and method
tips: Use the date filter to reconcile daily collections
Payment history provides a complete audit trail for all transactions
warnings: Only correct receipts through the proper refund/credit note process
Never delete payment records — they are permanent for accounting

inbox.title: Inbox / Messages
inbox.where: Sidebar → Administration → Inbox (or the Bell icon in the top-right header)
steps: Click 'Inbox' from the sidebar for the full message list
OR click the 🔔 bell icon in the top header for a quick preview
View all system notifications and internal messages
Click on any notification to read it — unread messages are highlighted in blue
Click the checkmark to mark as read, or the trash icon to delete
Click 'Mark All as Read' at the top to clear all notifications
Notifications auto-arrive for events like: new lab results, new appointments, billing alerts
tips: Check your inbox at the start of every shift
Notifications with a link (blue text) will take you directly to the relevant page
warnings: Deleting a notification is permanent — make sure to note any important information first
System notifications are generated automatically — you cannot reply to them

staff.title: Staff Management
steps: Click 'Staff' from the sidebar
View all current staff members with their roles and status
Click '+ Invite Staff' to add a new staff member
Enter their name, email address, and select their role
Available roles: Hospital Admin, Reception, Laboratory, Pharmacist, Nurse, Doctor, MD, Director
Click 'Send Invitation' — they will receive an email with login instructions
To update a staff member's role: click their name → Edit → change role → Save
To deactivate: click on the staff member → 'Deactivate'
tips: Assign the minimum necessary role — do not give everyone admin access
Deactivate instead of deleting when a staff member leaves — records are preserved
warnings: Admin role has full access to everything — assign carefully
Invitation emails expire after 48 hours — resend if not accepted in time

shareholders.title: Shareholders
steps: Click 'Shareholders' from the sidebar
View all shareholders with their ownership percentage and share value
Click '+ Add Shareholder' to register a new shareholder
Enter: name, contact, share percentage, investment amount
DISTRIBUTIONS: record dividend or profit distributions — click 'New Distribution' → select shareholder → enter amount
DIVIDENDS: process declared dividends — track paid vs. pending
View individual shareholder reports and their transaction history
tips: Keep shareholder contact information up to date for distribution communications
Generate shareholder reports at year-end for financial statements
warnings: Total shareholder percentage must always equal 100% — verify after adding new shareholders
Distributions must be approved by the Board before recording

multiBranch.title: Multi-Branch Management
steps: Click 'Multi-Branch' from the sidebar
View all branches of your hospital network
See performance metrics for each branch: revenue, patients, occupancy
Click on a branch to see its detailed performance
To add a new branch: go to Super Admin panel → register a new hospital
tips: Use the comparison view to identify top and underperforming branches
Each branch operates independently but data rolls up to this central view
warnings: Branch data is read-only here — changes must be made by logging into each branch
This feature shows aggregated data and may have a slight delay (up to 5 minutes)

reports.title: Reports
steps: Click 'Reports' from the sidebar for the reports overview
Choose a report type: Lab Reports, Pharmacy Reports, Appointment Reports
Set the date range for the report
Apply any filters (e.g. specific doctor, department, test type)
Click 'Generate Report'
View results in table or chart format
Click 'Export' to download as CSV or PDF
Print directly using your browser's print function
tips: Generate monthly reports for management review
Use Lab Reports to track high-volume tests and revenue
warnings: Large date ranges (more than 3 months) may take longer to load
Reports show data as of the current moment — export to preserve the snapshot

systemAudit.title: System Audit Log
steps: Click 'System Audit' from the sidebar
View a real-time log of all actions performed in the system
Each entry shows: who did it, what they did, when, and from which IP address
Use filters to search by user, action type, or date range
Click on any entry to see full details
Use this to investigate suspicious activity or track changes
tips: Regularly review the audit log to catch unauthorized access
Look for unusual login times or bulk data deletions
warnings: Audit logs cannot be edited or deleted — they are permanent records
Accessing this page is itself logged

website.title: Website / CMS
steps: Click 'Website' from the sidebar
Edit your hospital's public-facing website content
Update: About Us text, Services listed, Contact information, Photos
Add or remove departments shown on the website
Update doctor profiles visible on the website
Toggle sections visible/hidden using the toggle switches
Click 'Publish Changes' to make updates live on your website
View website analytics: visitors, page views, popular content
tips: Keep the website content updated — especially opening hours and doctor availability
Add high-quality photos to make a professional impression
warnings: Published changes go live immediately — review before publishing
Do not put patient information on the public website

aiAssistant.title: AI Assistant
steps: Click 'AI Assistant' from the sidebar
Type your question or request in the chat box
Examples: 'What is the top diagnosis in the last month?', 'Summarize today's admissions', 'Write a discharge summary for patient 123'
Press Enter or click Send
The AI will respond with analysis, suggestions, or generated text
Copy or use the AI's response as needed
tips: Be specific in your questions for better answers
Use the AI to help write consultation notes, discharge summaries, or reports
warnings: AI responses are suggestions — always review before using in clinical documents
Do not enter patient names or sensitive information unnecessarily

patientPortal.title: Patient Portal
steps: Click 'Patient Portal' from the sidebar
View what patients can see when they log into the patient portal
Patients can: view their appointments, lab results, prescriptions, bills, and medical history
From here you can manage patient portal access for specific patients
To grant portal access: find the patient → 'Grant Portal Access' → send login to patient's email
tips: Encourage patients to use the portal as it reduces phone inquiries
Ensure patient email addresses are correctly entered in their profile
warnings: Patients can only see their own information — privacy is maintained
Revoke portal access if a patient requests it or if their account is compromised

superAdmin.title: Super Admin Panel
steps: Log in with a Super Admin account
DASHBOARD: view platform-wide statistics (total hospitals, users, revenue)
HOSPITALS: view all registered hospitals — click on one to see its details
ONBOARDING QUEUE: process new hospital sign-up requests
Click on a pending request → review details → 'Approve' or 'Reject'
AUDIT LOG: view all super admin actions across the entire platform
HEALTH: monitor the health of servers, databases, and API endpoints
SETTINGS: configure global platform settings
tips: Review the onboarding queue daily — hospitals are waiting for activation
Monitor the Health page for any service degradation
warnings: Super Admin has access to all hospitals — use this power responsibly
Never approve a hospital without verifying their registration documents

triageChatbot.title: Triage Chatbot
steps: Navigate to the Triage Chatbot page
The chatbot guides patients through a series of symptom questions
Patients answer Yes/No or choose from options
Based on answers, the system suggests: 'Go to Emergency', 'Book an Appointment', 'Home Care'
Reception staff can run through the chatbot on behalf of walk-in patients
The chatbot result is logged in the patient's record
tips: Use the triage chatbot for walk-in patients to quickly assess urgency
The chatbot is based on validated clinical decision trees
warnings: The chatbot is a screening tool — it does not replace doctor assessment
Always escalate to a doctor if in doubt about patient condition

roleDashboards.title: Role-Based Dashboards
roleDashboards.where: Automatically shown after login based on your role
steps: HOSPITAL ADMIN DASHBOARD: full overview of all operations, metrics, quick actions
DOCTOR DASHBOARD: today's appointments, pending prescriptions, recent patients, lab results to review
RECEPTION DASHBOARD: appointments today, patient check-ins, pending billing, quick registration
MD DASHBOARD: financial KPIs, profit/loss summary, staff performance overview
DIRECTOR DASHBOARD: governance metrics, shareholder summary, strategic indicators
PHARMACIST DASHBOARD: pending dispenses, low stock alerts, recent prescriptions
LAB DASHBOARD: pending test orders, results to be entered, completed tests today
tips: Your dashboard is customized for your role — focus on the widgets most relevant to your work
Dashboard data refreshes automatically every few minutes
warnings: If your dashboard shows the wrong role, contact your Hospital Admin
Dashboard numbers are summaries — use specific module pages for detailed information
